007e7d6cb64a7dfcdcad9fc6f4f0ba69fdc88203: LLMs currently rely heavily on cloud computing, leading to long response times, high bandwidth costs, and significant privacy concerns.
Existing edge computing solutions for LLMs (e.g., quantization, cloud-edge collaboration) have limitations such as accuracy loss or high/unstable latency.
The proposed EdgeShard framework enables efficient collaborative LLM inference by partitioning models across heterogeneous edge devices and cloud servers.
EdgeShard includes a dynamic programming algorithm for joint device selection and model partition, optimizing for either inference latency or throughput.
Experimental results show EdgeShard significantly reduces latency (up to 50%) and doubles throughput compared to on-device and vertical cloud-edge methods.
929305892d4ddae575a0fc23227a8139f7681632: State-of-the-art LLMs (GPT-4, Claude v1.3) remain vulnerable to adversarial misuse via jailbreak attacks, despite extensive safety training and red-teaming efforts.
Two primary failure modes—competing objectives and mismatched generalization—underlie these vulnerabilities and successfully guide the design of new attacks.
New attacks leveraging these failure modes significantly outperform existing ad hoc jailbreaks, succeeding on over 96% of evaluated prompts, including 100% of curated red-teaming prompts.
The analysis highlights the necessity of safety mechanisms achieving "safety-capability parity" with the underlying model.
Scaling alone is insufficient to resolve these safety failure modes and may even exacerbate issues like mismatched generalization.
